channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Crypto market slips even as equities advance. Pump surges on social-media chatter

item.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and Trend ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description:
A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet.
The f...

item.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
item.description: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]...

item.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description:
Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov.
His wife, Maria Y...

item.title: CLARITY Act Could End ‘Coin Flip’ Crypto Enforcement, Helium Legal Chief Says
item.description: Nova Labs’ legal chief said the CLARITY Act could stop crypto regulation from reversing whenever US political leadership changes. The SEC dismissed its digital asset ......

item.title: Windows Server 2022 reach end of mainstream support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced that Windows Server 2022 will reach the mainstream end date in October 2026, but will switch to extended support and continue receiving security updates for five more years. [...]...
